title: Arenas, Pools, Slabs, and Fragmentation · C Memory Workbench
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working instrumented arena and object pool while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of instrumented arena and object pool while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Implement checked alignment and bump arithmetic with capacity, high-water, reset, and out-of-memory evidence.
Bind every arena allocation to a phase or owner whose reset cannot race with surviving borrows.
Maintain free-list membership, generation, duplicate-release, and exhaustion invariants.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Arena Bump Invariant
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 6254. Produce bounded C evidence to maintain checked arena offset and high-water invariants; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Implement checked alignment and bump arithmetic with capacity, high-water, reset, and out-of-memory evidence.

Produce bounded C evidence to maintain checked arena offset and high-water invariants; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure internal and external fragmentation, peak live bytes, reserved bytes, churn, and locality before redesign.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Fragmentation Measurement, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: RSS alone identifies allocator fragmentation.

stimulus.title: Fragmentation Measurement failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure internal and external fragmentation, peak live bytes, reserved bytes, churn, and locality before redesign.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Fragmentation Measurement case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Fragmentation Measurement condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Fragmentation Measurement
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Fragmentation Measurement: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 2825. Produce bounded C evidence to maintain checked arena offset and high-water invariants; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure internal and external fragmentation, peak live bytes, reserved bytes, churn, and locality before redesign.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Fragmentation Measurement.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Fragmentation Measurement, keep Fragmentation Measurement connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Measure internal and external fragmentation, peak live bytes, reserved bytes, churn, and locality before redesign.
